onboarding.step8_desc: The heart of the app is an LSTM neural network. It's an advanced algorithm that learns your trends, dosing, and meal reactions.
onboarding.disclaimer: I understand that the application does not replace medical advice and I accept the risks associated with its use.
onboarding.step10_desc: GlikoControl IS NOT a medical device. The application is for informational and educational purposes only. Never make insulin dosing or diet change decisions solely based on app data. Any therapy changes must be consulted with a diabetologist.
onboarding.step4_desc: If the difference between your sensor (CGM) and glucometer is significant, you can enter a fingerstick result in your profile (Hardware section), and GlikoControl will correct subsequent readings.
onboarding.step2_desc: Manage meals easier than ever. Use the Meal Plate to quickly sum up carbs and proteins from products, and the app will calculate the exchanges for you.
onboarding.step1_desc: This is a fan project created for us - diabetics. No unnecessary pathos, just a tool designed to make our life with diabetes easier.
onboarding.step9_desc: For the youngest (and those young at heart!), we have Gliko. Take care of your blood sugar, and your virtual pet will grow, level up, and get new outfits.
onboarding.language_desc: Choose the language you want to use in the app. You can change this at any time in the settings.
onboarding.step5_desc: We often forget that temperature and pressure heavily affect our blood sugar. GlikoControl has built-in weather indicators that will warn you of increased hypo or hyper risks.
onboarding.step7_desc: We use GlikoSense technology, which analyzes your blood sugar directly on your phone (offline). No sensitive trend data leaves your device.
onboarding.step3_desc: After taking insulin, log it in the Diary or using the Calculator. This will help algorithms calculate your Insulin on Board (IOB) and prevent dose stacking.
onboarding.step6_desc: Your medical data is sacred. It is NOT processed for advertising purposes or shared with anyone without your explicit consent. Everything is encrypted and secure.

bolus.title: Dose Calculator
bolus.ask_ai: 🤖 Ask AI to adjust dose based on history
bolus.timing_hypo: 🔴 HYPO! Eat carbs. Do not administer bolus!
bolus.timing_normal: 🟢 NORMAL. Bolus and eat in 5-10 min.
bolus.timing_very_high: 🟠 VERY HIGH! Wait at least 30 min.
bolus.timing_critical: 🔴 CRITICAL! Administer correction only and wait before eating.
bolus.reminder_body: {{minutes}} minutes have passed since the bolus. Your glucose should be starting to drop.
bolus.behavior_fast_carb: Carb-heavy meal, fast absorption. May cause sudden glucose spike. Recommended to administer insulin several minutes before eating.
bolus.behavior_pizza: High protein and fat content (Pizza-type). Absorption strongly delayed. Extended or combo bolus recommended over several hours.
bolus.behavior_high_fat: Fat proportion will delay the carb glucose peak. Monitor blood sugar 2-4 hours later.
bolus.behavior_high_protein: Elevated protein content. May result in late, slow glucose rise due to gluconeogenesis.
bolus.info_text: Calculation includes <1>Insulin on Board (IOB)</1>, <2>Carbs on Board (COB)</2>, personal ratios and trend. You can use the camera to scan meals.
bolus.err_empty: Enter a dose, carbs or blood sugar
bolus.scan_overloaded: AI servers are overloaded. Please try again.
bolus.alc_warn_beer: Beer initially raises blood sugar (approx. 15g carbs/500ml), but alcohol will cause a drop later. Dose reduced preventively by 30%. Be careful at night!
bolus.alc_warn_wine: Dry wine has almost no carbs (approx. 2g), but alcohol will slowly block the liver. Dose significantly reduced.
bolus.alc_warn_vodka: Pure alcohol strongly blocks hepatic glucose release. VERY HIGH RISK of severe hypoglycemia at night! Best to eat something without insulin coverage.
bolus.alc_warn_drink: Juices and syrups in cocktails will spike glucose quickly, then alcohol will lower it later. Dose reduced by 30%. Watch for overnight drops.
bolus.info_suffix: , personal ratios and trend. You can use the camera to scan meals.

auto.dla_wiekszosci_pacjentow_z_cuk:
For most patients with diabetes:
- Fasting and before meals: 70–110 mg/dL.
- 2 hours after meals: < 140 mg/dL.
- Time in target range (TIR 70-180 mg/dL): at least 70% of the time.
- HbA1c (glycated hemoglobin): ≤ 7% (in selected groups and in pregnant women, the standards are more strict).
auto.poziom_cukru_70_mg_dl_dla_pacj:
Blood sugar level < 70 mg/dL (for healthy patients the norms are lower, for diabetics this is the alarm threshold).
Symptoms: sweating, hand tremors, rapid heartbeat, confusion, intense hunger.
Treatment (15/15 Rule): Eat/drink 15g of simple carbohydrates (e.g., half a glass of juice, 3 glucose tablets). Wait 15 minutes and measure blood sugar. Repeat if still < 70 mg/dL.
auto.hiperglikemia_to_przedluzajacy:
Hyperglycemia is a prolonged high blood sugar level (e.g., >200 mg/dL). If the sugar exceeds 250 mg/dL and is accompanied by symptoms (abdominal pain, nausea, dry mouth, frequent urination), CHECK KETONES in the blood or urine.  
Lack of insulin causes fat burning and the release of ketone bodies. Ketoacidosis is a life-threatening condition requiring rapid hydration and insulin administration.
auto.wskaznik_odzwierciedlajacy_sre:
An indicator reflecting the average blood glucose concentration over the last approximately 3 months. The result is given in %.  
Ideally, for most young adults, this is below 7%, and in type 1 diabetes, it is often aimed at <6.5%.  
HbA1c correlates with the risk of developing chronic diabetic complications.
auto.1_g_weglowodanow_przyswajalnyc:
1 g of absorbable carbohydrates raises blood sugar quickly. For calculation purposes, Carbohydrate Exchanges (CE) are often used: 1 CE = 10 g of carbohydrates.  
1 Protein-Fat Exchange (PFE) = 100 kcal from protein and fat. It is absorbed more slowly (up to several hours), which is why prolonged/complex boluses are used on the pump for fatty and protein-rich meals (e.g., pizza).
auto.indeks_glikemiczny_ig_mowi_o_t: Glycemic Index (GI) indicates how QUICKLY a given product raises blood sugar (0-100). Low GI < 55, High > 70. Glycemic Load (GL) also takes into account portion size, giving a better picture of a meal's impact on blood sugar. Avoid foods with high GI and GL on an empty stomach.
auto.w_cukrzycy_typu_1_podanie_insu:
In type 1 diabetes, the administration of rapid-acting insulin requires a waiting period ('pre-bolus') before it starts to work, in order to match the peak of insulin action with absorption from the intestines.  
For high GI: 15-20 minutes. For medium GI: 10-15 minutes. For low GI or protein-fat meals: immediately before or after the meal (depending on the starting blood sugar level).
auto.zasada_dzialania_insuliny_bazo: The principle of basal and bolus insulin action
auto.baza_insulina_ciagla_odpowiada:
Basal: Continuous insulin, responsible for covering glucose release by the liver between meals and at night (administered in micro fractional doses every few minutes with a pump, with a pen as a long-acting injection). 
Meal bolus (for food): covers carbohydrates and possibly residual blood sugar from the meal. 
Correction bolus: insulin dose that lowers 'bad', too high blood sugar to the target level.
auto.wysilek_tlenowy_aerobowy_np_bi:
Oxygen exercise (aerobic: e.g., running, cycling) burns sugar and usually strongly lowers blood glucose. It requires carbohydrate supplementation beforehand or a reduction of basal insulin.  
Anaerobic exercise (anaerobic: sprinting, strength training) causes an adrenaline surge and can cause a sudden increase in blood glucose in some people, as well as sudden drops long after exercise.
auto.wplyw_stresu_upalu_i_infekcji: The impact of stress, heat, and infections
auto.infekcja_przeziebienie_goraczk:
Infection (cold, fever) almost always increases the need for insulin (disease-related insulin resistance) by 20-50% due to the immune system's response.  
Stress: a surge of cortisol causes insulin resistance, resulting in so-called 'stress sugars.'  
Heat and hot baths: improve skin blood flow, which accelerates the absorption and action of insulin (risk of hypoglycemia).
auto.zasady_rotacji_miejsc_wklucia: Rules for rotation of insertion sites
auto.insuline_podaje_sie_do_tkanki:
Insulin is administered into the subcutaneous tissue. Regular rotation of injection sites (abdomen, thighs, buttocks, arms) prevents the formation of adhesions and lipohypertrophy.  
Injecting into adhesions causes insulin to be absorbed poorly and very slowly, which is a common cause of 'spikes' and unexplained fluctuations in blood sugar.
auto.efekt_brzasku_naturalny_uwarun:
Dawn effect: Natural, hormonally conditioned (growth hormone, cortisol) increase in blood glucose in the early morning hours (usually 3:00 - 8:00), without prior hypoglycemia.  
Somogyi phenomenon (rebound phenomenon): Morning hyperglycemia being the body’s defensive reaction to undetected, deep hypoglycemia that triggers stress hormone release at night. It requires lowering the nighttime basal rate, unlike the dawn effect, which requires increasing it.
auto.hormon_przeciwstawny_do_insuli:
A hormone opposite to insulin. Used in severe hypoglycemia when the patient is unconscious, has seizures, or cannot swallow. It releases glucose stored in the liver (glycogen). 
How to use: Follow the instructions (intramuscular injection or nasal powder like Baqsimi). After waking up and improving condition (when able to swallow), eat complex carbohydrates to replenish liver stores (risk of recurrent hypoglycemia).
auto.alkohol_szczegolnie_wysokoproc:
Alcohol (especially high-proof) blocks the release of glucose by the liver, which DRAMATICALLY INCREASES THE RISK OF SEVERE HYPOGLYCEMIA. This effect can last for up to several hours after consumption.  
Never drink alcohol on an empty stomach. Before going to bed, make sure you have eaten an extra portion of complex carbohydrates to prevent a nighttime drop. Important: glucagon may not work during alcohol intoxication, as the liver is busy metabolizing alcohol.
auto.wplyw_hormonow_plciowych_a_gli: The impact of sex hormones on blood glucose
auto.kobiety_w_2_fazie_cyklu_np_1_2: Women in the 2nd phase of the cycle (e.g., 1-2 weeks before their period) struggle with high progesterone, which causes insulin resistance. The need for insulin can increase by as much as 20-30%. On the 1st and 2nd day of the cycle, the need drastically and sharply decreases – there is a high risk of hypoglycemia!
auto.tluszcze_i_bialka_opozniaja_op: Fats and proteins delay stomach emptying, which slows down carbohydrate absorption. Meals with a high amount of them (pizza, fast food) cause glucose to be released into the blood even up to 8 hours after eating. This usually requires the use of an extended or mixed bolus step by step.
auto.blonnik_wlokno_pokarmowe_nie_u: Fiber (dietary fiber) is not digested or absorbed in the digestive tract. Consuming foods rich in fiber (vegetables, whole grain bread, oatmeal) significantly lowers the total Glycemic Load of a meal and prevents rapid spikes in blood sugar after eating.
auto.zapas_insuliny_zawsze_przechow:
We ALWAYS store insulin in the refrigerator (2-8°C). It must not be frozen!  
An opened pen (injector) with insulin or a vial used for a pump is kept at room temperature (up to 25-30°C) and usually must be used within 28 days (or 4 weeks). Insulin exposed to frost or heat loses its properties and becomes water.
auto.sensory_np_dexcom_freestyle_li: Sensors (e.g., Dexcom, Freestyle Libre) measure sugar in the interstitial fluid (not in the blood). Therefore, there is a delay of about 5-15 minutes compared to a fingerstick measurement with a glucometer. Trend arrows indicate not only what your sugar level is now, but what it will be in 15-30 minutes.
auto.analogi_szybkodzialajace_np_no:
Rapid-acting analogs (e.g., Novorapid, Humalog, Fiasp, Lyumjev): start to act after about 5-15 minutes, reach peak action in 1-2 hours, and act completely for 3-4 hours.  
Long-acting analogs (e.g., Lantus, Levemir, Tresiba, Toujeo): act from several hours up to 42 hours at a very flat (peakless) level, serving as a basal injection from pens once or twice a day.
auto.do_samolotu_insuline_i_osprzet: On the plane: insulin and life-saving equipment should ALWAYS be carried in hand luggage (insulin would freeze in the luggage compartment). It is worth having a written medical certificate (in Polish and English). When crossing several time zones, the basal dose from pens must be appropriately reduced or adjusted, and meals should be aligned with the sleep-wake rhythm.

This allows your Neural Artificial Intelligence to notice trends in the future, such as whether frost raises blood sugar or whether you have more drops in hot weather.
auto.to_inteligentny_asystent_ai_na: This is an intelligent AI assistant permanently integrated into the application, optimized to support diabetes. It will answer your questions about diet or blood sugar management, and upon request, it quickly adds the meal you describe to the Meal Plate.
auto.glikosense_to_zaawansowana_sie: GlikoSense is an advanced neural network designed for in-depth analysis of your blood glucose history, administered insulin, and meals. This system reviews recorded logs and looks for relationships not immediately visible, helping, for example, to determine why glucose dangerously rises after certain meals.
auto.jak_automatycznie_dodawac_posi: How to automatically add meals using AI?
auto.wystarczy_ze_napiszesz_w_gliko: All you have to do is write in GlikoChat: 'Today I ate two slices of bread with butter, scrambled eggs with 3 eggs, and an apple.' The AI will automatically recognize these products, match their weights, divide them into carbohydrates, proteins, and fats, and then add the ready meal directly to your Meal Plate without a single click from you!
auto.do_czego_sluzy_talerz_posilkow: What is the Meal Plate used for?
auto.talerz_pozwala_komponowac_posi: The Plate allows you to compose meals from various ingredients and build your own list of dishes. The Plate mechanism automatically converts the arranged products into carbohydrate exchanges (CE) and protein-fat exchanges (PFE) so that you can use this data in the system's Bolus Calculator without additional rewriting.
auto.po_co_ustala_sie_opoznienie_w: Why is a delay established in the Meal Plate?
auto.tlustsze_potrawy_z_duza_ilosci: Fattier dishes with a high amount of protein (e.g., pizza, cheese) slow down the absorption of sugars, so the so-called PTF (protein-fat exchanges) take hours to digest. The plate takes the release time into account. We can anticipate the prolongation and calculate the appropriate dose for the pump (extended or dual bolus).
auto.jak_dziala_szczegolowo_kalkula: How does the Bolus Calculator work in detail?
auto.kalkulator_opiera_sie_o_sprawd: The calculator is based on a proven formula. It uses your settings (ISF / Carbohydrate Ratio). It first calculates the glucose correction: (Glucose - Target) / ISF. To this, it adds insulin for the meal entered on the Plate based on the I:C ratio (insulin to carbohydrates) and subtracts the estimated Active Insulin (IOB) to avoid overlapping doses and potential hypoglycemia.
auto.gdzie_zmienic_moje_wazne_wspol: Where can I change my important coefficients (ISF, Goals)?
auto.kliknij_przycisk_wiecej_menu_n: Click the 'More' button (menu) at the bottom right of the navigation and select the 'Therapy' option. There you will find fields to enter your current insulin sensitivity (ISF), your carbohydrate-to-insulin ratio (CR/ICR), target glucose level, and IOB absorption parameters.
auto.nie_musisz_robic_tego_recznie: You don't have to do this manually. If you have a CGM, go to 'More' and then to 'Integrations (API)'. There, attach your Nightscout link, and the app will automatically start fetching glucose readings minute by minute in the background to your screen (and for the Calculator / AI).
auto.po_co_sa_monety_i_zwierzak_sys: What are coins and the Pet (Gamification System) for?
auto.by_zachecic_w_szczegolnosci_ml: To encourage (especially young people) to diligently monitor their entries, the system rewards special coins for each logging of results, meal creation, using AI, or reading summaries. In 'More -> Shop' you can exchange them for customizations and outfits for your local virtual pet.
auto.jak_dzialaja_diety_w_aplikacji: How do Diets work in the app?
auto.sekcja_diet_dostepna_w_menu_wi: The Diet section, available in the 'More' menu, supports the development of healthy habits. You will find there an interactive list of goals, such as avoiding sweets, drinking water, or physical activity for a given day. The system tracks the achievement of these goals, and you gain better control over your weight, which in the long term reduces insulin resistance and stabilizes blood sugar levels.

Oxygen exercise (aerobic: e.g., running, cycling) burns sugar and usually strongly lowers blood glucose. It requires carbohydrate supplementation beforehand or a reduction of basal insulin.  
Anaerobic exercise (anaerobic: sprinting, strength training) causes an adrenaline surge and can cause a sudden increase in blood glucose in some people, as well as sudden drops long after exercise.

auto.zmniejsz_baze_o_30_50_min: Reduce the base by 30-50% at least 1.5 hours before departure.

auto.do_czego_sluzy_talerz_pos: What is the Meal Plate used for?

auto.po_co_ustala_sie_opoznien: Why is a delay established in the Meal Plate?

The plate takes the release time into account. We can anticipate the prolongation and calculate the appropriate dose for the pump (extended or dual bolus).
auto.jak_dziala_szczegolowo_ka: How does the Bolus Calculator work in detail?
auto.kalkulator_opiera_sie_o_s: The calculator is based on a proven formula. It uses your settings (ISF / Carbohydrate Ratio). It first calculates the glucose correction: (Glucose - Target) / ISF. To this, it adds insulin for the meal entered on the Plate based on the I:C ratio (insulin to carbohydrates) and subtracts the estimated Active Insulin (IOB) to avoid overlapping doses and potential hypoglycemia.

auto.dla_wiekszosci_pacjentow:
For most patients with diabetes:
- Fasting and before meals: 70–110 mg/dL.
- 2 hours after meals: < 140 mg/dL.
- Time in target range (TIR 70-180 mg/dL): at least 70% of the time.
- HbA1c (glycated hemoglobin): ≤ 7% (in selected groups and in pregnant women, the standards are more strict).
auto.poziom_cukru_70_mg_dl_dla:
Blood sugar level < 70 mg/dL (for healthy patients the norms are lower, for diabetics this is the alarm threshold).
Symptoms: sweating, hand tremors, rapid heartbeat, confusion, intense hunger.
Treatment (15/15 Rule): Eat/drink 15g of simple carbohydrates (e.g., half a glass of juice, 3 glucose tablets). Wait 15 minutes and measure blood sugar. Repeat if still < 70 mg/dL.
auto.hiperglikemia_to_przedluz:
Hyperglycemia is a prolonged high blood sugar level (e.g., >200 mg/dL). If the sugar exceeds 250 mg/dL and is accompanied by symptoms (abdominal pain, nausea, dry mouth, frequent urination), CHECK KETONES in the blood or urine.  
Lack of insulin causes fat burning and the release of ketone bodies. Ketoacidosis is a life-threatening condition requiring rapid hydration and insulin administration.
auto.wskaznik_odzwierciedlajac:
An indicator reflecting the average blood glucose concentration over the last approximately 3 months. The result is given in %.  
Ideally, for most young adults it is below 7%, and in type 1 diabetes, it is often aimed to be <6.5%.  
HbA1c correlates with the risk of developing chronic diabetic complications.
auto.1_g_weglowodanow_przyswaj:
1 g of absorbable carbohydrates raises blood sugar quickly. For calculation purposes, Carbohydrate Exchanges (CE) are often used: 1 CE = 10 g of carbohydrates.  
1 Protein-Fat Exchange (PFE) = 100 kcal from proteins and fats. It is absorbed more slowly (up to several hours), which is why for fatty and protein-rich meals (e.g., pizza), extended/dual boluses are used on the pump.
auto.indeks_glikemiczny_ig_mow: Glycemic Index (GI) indicates how QUICKLY a given product raises blood sugar (0-100). Low GI < 55, High > 70. Glycemic Load (GL) also takes into account portion size, giving a better picture of a meal's impact on blood sugar. Avoid foods with high GI and GL on an empty stomach.
auto.w_cukrzycy_typu_1_podanie:
In type 1 diabetes, the administration of rapid-acting insulin requires a waiting period ('pre-bolus') before it starts to work, in order to align the peak of insulin action with absorption from the intestines.  
For high GI: 15-20 minutes. For medium GI: 10-15 minutes. For low GI or protein-fat meals: immediately before or after the meal (depending on the starting blood sugar level).
auto.zasada_dzialania_insuliny: The principle of basal and bolus insulin action
auto.baza_insulina_ciagla_odpo:
Basal: Continuous insulin, responsible for covering glucose release by the liver between meals and at night (administered in micro fractional doses every few minutes with a pump, with a pen as a long-acting injection). 
Meal bolus (for food): covers carbohydrates and possibly residual bolus from the meal. 
Correction bolus: insulin dose that lowers 'bad', too high blood sugar to the target level.
auto.wplyw_stresu_upalu_i_infe: The impact of stress, heat, and infection
auto.infekcja_przeziebienie_go: Infection (cold, fever) almost always increases insulin requirements (disease-related insulin resistance) by 20-50% due to the immune system's fight. Stress: a cortisol surge causes insulin resistance, resulting in so-called “stress sugars.” Heat and hot baths: improve blood flow to the skin, which accelerates insulin absorption and action (risk of hypoglycemia).
auto.insuline_podaje_sie_do_tk:
Insulin is administered into the subcutaneous tissue. Regular rotation of injection sites (abdomen, thighs, buttocks, arms) prevents the formation of adhesions and lipohypertrophy.  
Injecting into adhesions causes insulin to be absorbed poorly and very slowly, which is a common cause of 'spikes' and unexplained fluctuations in blood sugar.
auto.efekt_brzasku_naturalny_u:
Dawn effect: Natural, hormonally conditioned (growth hormone, cortisol) increase in blood glucose in the early morning hours (usually 3:00 - 8:00), without prior hypoglycemia.  
Somogyi phenomenon (rebound phenomenon): Morning hyperglycemia being the body’s defensive reaction to undetected, deep hypoglycemia that triggers stress hormone release at night. It requires lowering the nighttime basal rate, unlike the dawn effect, which requires increasing it.
auto.hormon_przeciwstawny_do_i:
Hormone opposite to insulin. Used in severe hypoglycemia, when the patient is unconscious, has seizures, or cannot swallow. Releases glucose stored in the liver (glycogen). 
How to use: Follow the instructions (intramuscular injection or nasal powder like Baqsimi). After waking up and improving condition (when able to swallow), eat complex carbohydrates to replenish liver stores (risk of recurrent hypoglycemia).
auto.alkohol_szczegolnie_wysok:
Alcohol (especially high-proof) blocks the release of glucose by the liver, which DRAMATICALLY INCREASES THE RISK OF SEVERE HYPOGLYCEMIA. This effect can last for up to several hours after consumption.  
Never drink alcohol on an empty stomach. Before going to bed, make sure you have eaten an extra portion of complex carbohydrates to prevent a nighttime drop. Important: glucagon may not work during alcohol intoxication, as the liver is busy metabolizing alcohol.
auto.wplyw_hormonow_plciowych: The impact of sex hormones on blood sugar
auto.kobiety_w_2_fazie_cyklu_n: Women in the 2nd phase of the cycle (e.g., 1-2 weeks before their period) struggle with high progesterone, which causes insulin resistance. The need for insulin can increase by as much as 20-30%. On the 1st and 2nd day of the cycle, the need drastically and sharply decreases – there is a high risk of hypoglycemia!
auto.tluszcze_i_bialka_opoznia: Fats and proteins delay stomach emptying, which slows down carbohydrate absorption. Meals high in them (pizza, fast food) cause glucose to be released into the blood up to 8 hours after eating. This most often requires the use of an extended or mixed bolus step by step.
auto.blonnik_wlokno_pokarmowe: Fiber (dietary fiber) is not digested or absorbed in the digestive tract. Consuming foods rich in fiber (vegetables, whole grain bread, oatmeal) significantly lowers the total Glycemic Load of a meal and prevents rapid spikes in blood sugar after eating.
auto.zapas_insuliny_zawsze_prz:
We ALWAYS store insulin in the refrigerator (2-8°C). It must not be frozen!  
An opened pen (injector) with insulin or a vial used for a pump is kept at room temperature (up to 25-30°C) and usually must be used within 28 days (or 4 weeks). Insulin exposed to frost or heat loses its properties and becomes water.
auto.sensory_np_dexcom_freesty: Sensors (e.g., Dexcom, Freestyle Libre) measure sugar in the interstitial fluid (not in the blood). Therefore, there is a delay of about 5-15 minutes compared to a fingerstick measurement with a glucometer. Trend arrows indicate not only what your sugar level is now, but what it will be in 15-30 minutes.
auto.analogi_szybkodzialajace:
Rapid-acting analogs (e.g., Novorapid, Humalog, Fiasp, Lyumjev): start to act after about 5-15 minutes, reach peak action in 1-2 hours and act completely for 3-4 hours.  
Long-acting analogs (e.g., Lantus, Levemir, Tresiba, Toujeo): act from several hours up to 42 hours at a very flat (peakless) level, serving as a base injected from pens once or twice a day.
auto.do_samolotu_insuline_i_os: On the plane: insulin and life-saving equipment should ALWAYS be carried in hand luggage (insulin would freeze in the luggage compartment). It is worth having a written medical certificate (in Polish and English). When crossing several time zones, the basal dose from pens needs to be appropriately shortened or adjusted, and food should be adapted to the sleep-wake rhythm.

auto.dla_wiekszosci_pacjentow_z_cuk:
For most diabetes patients:
- Fasting and before meals: 70–110 mg/dL.
- 2 hours after meal: < 140 mg/dL.
- Time in Range (TIR 70-180 mg/dL): min. 70% of time.
- HbA1c (glycated hemoglobin): <= 7% (in selected groups and pregnant women norms are stricter).
auto.poziom_cukru_70_mg_dl_dla_pacj:
Sugar level < 70 mg/dL (for healthy patients norms are lower, for diabetics this is an alarm limit).
Symptoms: sweating, shaking hands, fast heartbeat, confusion, huge hunger.
Treatment (15/15 Rule): Eat/drink 15g of simple carbs (e.g., half a glass of juice, 3 glucose tablets). Wait 15 minutes and check sugar. Repeat if still < 70 mg/dL.
auto.hiperglikemia_to_przedluzajacy:
Hyperglycemia is prolonged high sugar level (e.g., >200 mg/dL). If sugar exceeds 250 mg/dL and is accompanied by symptoms (stomach ache, nausea, dry mouth, constant urination), CHECK KETONES from blood or urine.
Lack of insulin causes fat burning and release of ketone bodies. Ketoacidosis is a life-threatening condition requiring rapid hydration and insulin administration.
auto.wskaznik_odzwierciedlajacy_sre:
An indicator reflecting the average blood glucose concentration over the last approx. 3 months. The result is given in %. 
The target for most young adults is below 7%, and in type 1 diabetes the goal is often <6.5%. 
HbA1c correlates with the risk of chronic diabetic complications.
auto.1_g_weglowodanow_przyswajalnyc:
1 g of digestible carbohydrates raises sugar quickly. For calculation purposes, Carbohydrate Exchanges (CE) are often used: 1 CE = 10g of carbohydrates.
1 Fat-Protein Unit (FPU) = 100 kcal derived from fats and proteins. It absorbs longer (up to several hours), therefore for fatty and protein-rich meals (e.g., pizza) prolonged/dual-wave boluses are used on a pump.
auto.indeks_glikemiczny_ig_mowi_o_t:
Glycemic Index (GI) tells you how QUICKLY a product raises blood sugar (0-100). Low GI < 55, High > 70.
Glycemic Load (GL) additionally considers portion size, giving a better picture of the meal's impact on glycemia. Avoid high GI and GL foods on an empty stomach.
auto.w_cukrzycy_typu_1_podanie_insu:
In type 1 diabetes, administering rapid-acting insulin requires waiting ('pre-bolus') before it starts working, to align the insulin peak with intestinal absorption.
For high GI: 15-20 min. For medium GI: 10-15 min. For low GI or fat-protein meals: right before or after the meal (depending on starting sugar level).
auto.zasada_dzialania_insuliny_bazo: Principles of basal and bolus insulin
auto.baza_insulina_ciagla_odpowiada:
Basal: Continuous insulin covering glucose release by the liver between meals and at night (in a pump, given in micro-doses every few minutes; from a pen, as a long-acting injection).
Meal bolus: covers carbohydrates and fat/protein from the meal.
Correction bolus: insulin dose lowering 'bad', too-high sugar to the target level.
auto.wysilek_tlenowy_aerobowy_np_bi:
Aerobic exercise (e.g., running, cycling) burns sugar and usually strongly lowers glycemia. It requires carb protection beforehand or reducing basal insulin.
Anaerobic exercise (sprint, strength) causes adrenaline release and can cause a sudden sugar spike in some people, followed by sudden drops long after exercise.
auto.wplyw_stresu_upalu_i_infekcji: Impact of stress, heat, and infection
auto.infekcja_przeziebienie_goraczk:
Infection (cold, fever) almost always increases insulin demand (illness insulin resistance) by 20-50% due to the immune system fighting.
Stress: cortisol release causes insulin resistance, resulting in 'stress sugars'.
Heat and hot baths: improve skin circulation, which speeds up insulin absorption and action (hypoglycemia risk).
auto.insuline_podaje_sie_do_tkanki:
Insulin is given into subcutaneous tissue. Regular rotation of injection sites (abdomen, thighs, buttocks, arms) prevents lipohypertrophy and scar tissue.
Injecting into scar tissue causes insulin to absorb poorly and very slowly, which is a common cause of 'bouncing' and unexplained sugar fluctuations.
auto.efekt_brzasku_naturalny_uwarun:
Dawn phenomenon: A natural, hormonally driven (growth hormone, cortisol) rise in glycemia in the early morning hours (usually 3:00 - 8:00 AM), without prior hypoglycemia.
Somogyi effect (rebound effect): Morning hyperglycemia acting as the body's defense reaction to undetected, deep hypoglycemia forcing the release of stress hormones at night. Requires lowering the night basal, unlike the dawn phenomenon which requires increasing it.
auto.hormon_przeciwstawny_do_insuli:
A counter-regulatory hormone to insulin. Used in severe hypoglycemia when the patient is unconscious, seizing, or cannot swallow. It releases glucose stored in the liver (glycogen).
How to use: Follow instructions (intramuscular injection or nasal powder like Baqsimi). After waking up and improving (when able to swallow), eat complex carbs to replenish liver reserves (risk of recurring hypoglycemia).
auto.alkohol_szczegolnie_wysokoproc:
Alcohol (especially high-proof) blocks the liver from releasing glucose, which DRASTICALLY INCREASES THE RISK OF SEVERE HYPOGLYCEMIA. This effect can last up to a dozen hours after drinking.
Never drink alcohol on an empty stomach. Before going to bed, make sure you ate an extra portion of complex carbs to prevent a night drop. Important: glucagon may not work during alcohol intoxication, as the liver is busy metabolizing alcohol.
auto.wplyw_hormonow_plciowych_a_gli: Impact of sex hormones on glycemia
auto.kobiety_w_2_fazie_cyklu_np_1_2:
Women in the 2nd phase of their cycle (e.g., 1-2 weeks before period) struggle with high progesterone, which causes insulin resistance. Insulin demand can increase by up to 20-30%.
On the 1st and 2nd day of the cycle, demand drops drastically and suddenly – there is a high risk of hypoglycemia!
auto.tluszcze_i_bialka_opozniaja_op: Fats and proteins delay stomach emptying, slowing down carb absorption. Meals with a large amount of them (pizza, fast food) cause glucose to be released into the blood even up to 8 hours after eating. This usually requires a prolonged or dual-wave/step-by-step bolus.
auto.blonnik_wlokno_pokarmowe_nie_u: Fiber (dietary fiber) is not digested or absorbed in the digestive tract. Eating fiber-rich foods (vegetables, whole grain bread, oatmeal) significantly lowers the meal's total Glycemic Load and prevents rapid sugar spikes after eating.
auto.zapas_insuliny_zawsze_przechow:
ALWAYS store backup insulin in the fridge (2-8°C). Do not freeze it!
An opened insulin pen or pump vial should be kept at room temperature (up to 25-30°C) and usually used within 28 days (or 4 weeks). Insulin exposed to freezing or extreme heat loses its properties and acts like water.
auto.sensory_np_dexcom_freestyle_li: Sensors (e.g., Dexcom, Freestyle Libre) measure sugar in interstitial fluid (not blood). Therefore, there is a 5-15 minute delay compared to a finger-prick blood meter. Trend arrows tell you not only what your sugar is now, but what it will be in 15-30 minutes.
auto.analogi_szybkodzialajace_np_no:
Rapid-acting analogs (e.g., Novorapid, Humalog, Fiasp, Lyumjev): start working after about 5-15 mins, peak at 1-2h, and finish acting within 3-4 hours.
Long-acting analogs (e.g., Lantus, Levemir, Tresiba, Toujeo): work from a dozen up to 42 hours at a very flat (peakless) level, acting as a basal dose injected from pens once or twice a day.
auto.do_samolotu_insuline_i_osprzet: On a plane: ALWAYS take insulin and life-saving equipment in carry-on baggage (insulin would freeze in the cargo hold). It is worth having a written medical certificate (in local and English language). When crossing several time zones, adjust or overlap basal pen doses, and align meals with the sleep-wake cycle.

auto.gdzie_wlacze_i_wylacze_widzet: Where can I turn the Widget and Weather Alerts on and off?
auto.sekcja_ustawien_profilu_w_zakl: The profile settings section in the 'System' tab has the 'Widget and Weather Alerts' option. This allows you to display a separate weather card on the main screen that gives you the latest data from outside the window. It also checks for extreme temperatures (such as severe frost or heat) and advises you on what may be the cause, e.g. faster insulin action.
auto.jak_dziala_pelna_integracja_po: How does the full integration of Weather in GlikoSense work?
auto.jezeli_rowniez_zakladce_system: If you also select the 'GlikoSense & Weather' switch in the 'System' tab, all newly saved sugars (especially those downloaded automatically, e.g. from the Nightscout cloud) will be 'stamped' with the temperature and pressure at a given moment. This allows your Neural Artificial Intelligence to notice a trend in the future whether frost raises sugar levels or whether you experience more lows in hot weather.
auto.to_inteligentny_asystent_ai_na: It is an intelligent AI assistant permanently embedded in the application, optimized for diabetes support. It will answer your questions about diet or glycemic management, and upon request, it will quickly add the meal you have described to the Meal Plate.
auto.glikosense_to_zaawansowana_sie: GlikoSense is an advanced neural network designed for deep analysis of the history of your glycemia, administered insulin and meals. This system reviews saved logs and looks for relationships that are invisible at first glance, helping, for example, to determine why sugar levels rise dangerously after certain meals.
auto.jak_automatycznie_dodawac_posi: How to automatically add meals via AI?
auto.wystarczy_ze_napiszesz_w_gliko: Just write in GlikoChat: 'Today I ate two slices of bread with butter, scrambled eggs with 3 eggs and an apple.' AI will recognize these products on its own, adjust their weights, divide them into carbohydrates, proteins and fats, and then add the ready meal directly to your Meal Plate without a single click on your part!
auto.do_czego_sluzy_talerz_posilkow: What is the Meal Plate used for?
auto.talerz_pozwala_komponowac_posi: The plate allows you to compose meals with various ingredients and build your own list of dishes. The Plate mechanism automatically converts the placed products into carbohydrate (WW) and protein-fat (WBT) exchangers, so that these data can be used in the system Bolus Calculator without additional rewriting.
auto.po_co_ustala_sie_opoznienie_w: Why is there a delay in the Meal Plate?
auto.tlustsze_potrawy_z_duza_ilosci: Fattier foods with a lot of protein (e.g. pizza, cheese) slow down the absorption of sugars, so the so-called WBT (protein-fat exchangers) take hours to digest. The plate takes into account the release time. We can predict the extension and calculate the appropriate dose for the pump (extended or complex bolus).
auto.jak_dziala_szczegolowo_kalkula: How does the Bolus Calculator work in detail?
auto.kalkulator_opiera_sie_o_sprawd: The calculator is based on a proven formula. Uses your findings (ISF / WW Factor). Calculates glucose correction first: (Glucose - Target) / ISF. Additionally, it adds insulin to the meal introduced into the Plate based on the I:C ratio (insulin to carbohydrates) and subtracts the estimated Active Insulin (IOB) to avoid overlapping doses and potential hypoglycemia.
auto.gdzie_zmienic_moje_wazne_wspol: Where to change my important factors (ISF, Goals)?
auto.kliknij_przycisk_wiecej_menu_n: Click the 'More' button (menu) at the bottom right navigation and select 'Therapy'. There you will find fields to enter your current insulin sensitivity (ISF), your WW/WBT ratio, target glucose level and IOB absorption parameters.
auto.nie_musisz_robic_tego_recznie: You don't have to do it manually. If you have CGM, go under 'More' to 'Integrations (API)'. There, pin your link to Nightscout, and the app in the background will start automatically pulling minute-by-minute glucose readings onto your screen (and for Calculator/AI use).
auto.po_co_sa_monety_i_zwierzak_sys: What are the coins and the Pet (Gamification System) for?
auto.by_zachecic_w_szczegolnosci_ml: To encourage (especially young people) to carefully monitor their entries, the system rewards you with special coins for each time you log results, create meals, use AI or read summaries. In 'More -> Shop' you can exchange them for modifications and clothes for your local virtual pet.
auto.jak_dzialaja_diety_w_aplikacji: How do Diets work in the app?
auto.sekcja_diet_dostepna_w_menu_wi: The Diet section, available in the 'More' menu, supports you in developing healthy habits. You will find an interactive list of goals, e.g. avoiding sweets, drinking water or physical activity for a given day. The system tracks the implementation of these assumptions, and you gain better control over your weight, which in the long run reduces insulin resistance and stabilizes glycemia.
